name: Hector
description: Hector, son of King Priam and Queen Hecuba of Troy, was the greatest of the Trojan warriors and a central figure in the Trojan War. Renowned for his courage, skill, and noble character, Hector was the leader of the Trojan army and the champion of his city. He was a devoted husband to Andromache and a loving father to Astyanax, torn between his duty to his family and his responsibility to defend Troy. Hector's most famous duel was with Achilles, the greatest of the Achaean warriors. Despite knowing his fate, Hector faced Achilles with unwavering resolve, ultimately falling in battle. His death marked a turning point in the war and solidified his place as a tragic hero in Greek mythology, embodying honor, duty, and the human cost of conflict. He is remembered for his bravery, his leadership, and his unwavering commitment to his people, even in the face of certain defeat. His death is mourned by both Trojans and some Achaeans, recognizing the loss of a truly great warrior and a noble man. He was respected even by his enemies, a testament to his character. He fought not for glory, but for his city and his family, making him a figure of enduring admiration and pathos.
subtitle: Troy's Greatest Warrior and Noble Hero
achievements: Briefly driving the Achaeans back to their ships
Upholding Trojan honor and defending his city

keyMyths.title: Hector's Farewell to Andromache
keyMyths.description: Hector bids farewell to his wife, Andromache, and son, Astyanax, knowing he must return to battle. This scene highlights his love for his family and his sense of duty.

keyMyths.title: The Death of Patroclus
keyMyths.description: Hector kills Patroclus, mistaking him for Achilles, which enrages Achilles and leads to Hector's ultimate downfall. This event is a pivotal moment in the Trojan War.

keyMyths.title: Hector's Duel with Achilles
keyMyths.description: Hector faces Achilles in single combat, knowing his fate. He fights valiantly but is ultimately defeated and killed by Achilles, sealing Troy's doom.

sources.author: Homer

sources.text: The primary source for Hector's story and the Trojan War.
